Manifestation/Magic by Holly black. Do you know how it feels to build magical energy?” the kelpie asked.
“It is a prickling feeling. Cup your hand and concentrate on building the
energy in it. What does it feel like?”
Kaye cupped her hand and imagined the air in her hand thickening and
shimmering with energy. After a moment, she looked up in surprise. “It
feels like when your hand falls asleep and then you move it. Prickly, like
you said, like little shocks of energy shooting through it. It hurts a little.”
“Move it back and forth between your hands. There you feel magic in
its raw state, ready to become whatever you want it to be.”
Kaye nodded, cradling the energy that was like a handful of nettles,
letting some of it trickle through her open fingers. It was a feeling she
remembered, sometimes coiling in her gut or pricking over her lips before
some strange thing happened.
“Now, how did you accomplish raising the energy? What did you do?”

Atlas feels like a mirror to me, his thoughts, fears and the way he looks down on himself, even though he’s a prodigy, are all familiar to me. 

Lucien reads as this confident, cocky yet genuine person, he has lots of empathy which I personally didn’t expect from him at the beginning which is surprising and refreshing. Usually this types of characters have cold souls.

Echoes of a Sunken Kingdom. 🏰🌿

Lost in the ruins of Croxden Abbey. There’s a silent alchemy in the way these red stones surrender to the grass. It’s the ultimate Solve et Coagula: the dissolution of human ambition into the eternal embrace of the Green Man. 🕯️✨

Walking through these arches feels like a slow, heavy doom metal riff—melancholic, ancient, and deeply grounded. Here, the veil between the ink of my studies and the raw spirit of the earth feels thin.

In every fallen brick, a lesson. In every shadow, a secret. 🌒🖋️ Croxden Abbey ruins dark academia nature goth Green Man aesthetic alchemy occultism memento mori historical places english heritage

The sickness of writing No desire to write. I write.
Resumption of two unknown worlds,
once, for so long a time, bound.
The disjunction is a complete thing,

but it will not change, it has never changed.
Magic, the current turned to stars
is the only pillow left to hold.
No, I have not changed. I always change

so as not to change at all. My soul
has never once been equal to itself.
The light weight of the meridian night
weighs upon my stomach.

I must complete, this is what demands
the conscience: what is once begun must
end. My end, a window full
of incomplete and unfinished tasks.

The sickness of writing, for as long
as it inhabits me, it pulls me by the neck
of thought and heart. It sets my sails
with the impulse of the storm wind’s gust.

When dawn comes, what calm
will it bring me? The dead man’s stillness.
And tumbling down among its cliffs,
I will have written yet another broken story. poets on tumblr writers on tumblr poetry literature dark academia existential dread melancho

Entry 258 - I think i’ve become one of the references

<!-- more --> Every researcher leaves traces behind. Annotations in the margins. Forgotten correspondence. A name buried in the bibliography that no one notices until years later. That’s normal.

What isn’t normal is finding your own name in a source published before you were born. I checked the date three times. Then the edition. Then the archive record. Everything was correct.

There, tucked into a footnote no longer than a sentence, was a reference to someone with my exact name studying the exact problem currently spread across my desk.

I should have been concerned. Instead, i kept reading because if history is already citing me, then somewhere ahead of me the work must be finished. I’m becoming increasingly worried that the conclusion already exists. dark academia literature poetry arg dreamscapefalls hidden pine pages pinefiles pine trees pinesparadox the author

Congratulations She told me “congrats!” when I told her in the future my name will change on the intake forms, she had assumed I was getting married, and I awkwardly laughed and replied 

“Oh, uh no, I mean, I’m getting a divorce” 

And she looked up and smiled and repeated the sentiment, “Then congrats again! Been there”

And I awkwardly laughed again, muttering something of a thanks and the usuals “yeah it’s so much paperwork” to which we both chuckled. 

But walking away, my smile faded, and my chest caved in. 

Because I didn’t want to leave my marriage.

I didn’t want to walk away.

Walking away felt like taking off my shoes and willingly walking across the knives edges when everything in me screamed not to. 

I loved with everything I had and everything I was. I didn’t leave because I no longer loved. 

I wanted it to work out so damn badly I would have carved out my own damn heart to fix whatever was broken between us. 

Because he had me so convinced that it was me.

8 dark Marie Antoinette facts most people never hear 👑

Marie Antoinette is usually remembered in two ways: the spoiled queen with the giant dresses, or the tragic woman who lost her head. The truth is that there’s a lot more to this young queen than most people ever get to hear.

👉 1. She was only 14 when she was sent to France to marry Louis XVI.

Marie Antoinette was not some fully formed political mastermind when she arrived at Versailles. She was a teenager used as a diplomatic bargaining chip.

It wasn’t about romance- her job was alliance, heirs, and survival inside one of the most judgmental courts in Europe.

👉 2. Before she became French, her identity was ‘rewritten’ to suit her new country.

When she crossed into France, Marie Antoinette went through a symbolic handover ceremony. The point was brutal but clear: she was leaving her old identity behind.

The Oracle Was Not Possessed

The Oracle of Delphi was not necessarily possessed. She was placed inside a system designed to thin the border between signal and symbol.

Ritual isolation. Repetition. Breath altered by subterranean fumes. A body held still long enough for ordinary perception to loosen. In occult language, this becomes prophecy. In psychological language, it resembles an altered state where image, memory, intuition, and association rise faster than the judging mind can suppress them.

This is why the Pythia still matters. Not because she proves the gods spoke, but because she exposes what the nervous system can do when it is stripped of noise and forced into threshold.

Modern life trains the opposite condition: interruption, vigilance, overstimulation, cognitive armor. We do not lack symbols. We lack the silence required to hear them.

La Fatigue et la Vie Je suis lasse. Ce mot paraît si léger parfois,
Tant de lèvres le disent sans en porter le poids.
Ils parlent de fatigue au détour d'une heure sombre,
Moi, je la vois grandir et m'habiter comme une ombre.

Je parle, et déjà mes paupières se referment,
Comme un livre oublié que le sommeil désarme.
Mon corps cède en silence, abandonnant le port,
Et jusqu'à mon souffle hésite encore.

J'ai voulu résister, lutter contre la chute,
Porter comme les autres ma part de route.
Mais le monde applaudit ceux qui serrent les dents,
Et regarde de travers ceux qui tombent pourtant.

Abandonner, dit-on, serait un péché ;
Il faut toujours courir, produire et s'arracher.
Alors je cache un peu ce naufrage intérieur,
Cette lente marée qui me vole mes heures.

Si tu pouvais entendre le grain de ma voix,
Tu y sentirais la nuit s'étendre pas à pas.
Une fatigue ancienne y pose son royaume,
Et transforme mes mots en fantômes.

Fatigué de parler, fatigué d'écouter,
Fatigué de chercher ce qu'i

Now they read differently. Less like sources. More like messages. Tiny annotations tucked beneath the main text. 

Remarks in the margins. Cryptic additions that seem unrelated until three chapters later when they suddenly become the most important thing on the page.

Tonight I followed a footnote to a reference that doesn’t exist anywhere in the catalog. No author. No title. Just a location.

A set of coordinates and beneath them, in handwriting that looked disturbingly familiar: if you’ve reached this citation, you’re already too close.

The contents were worse. Photographs I don’t remember being taken. Notes describing conversations I’d only had in private. Sketches of places I haven’t visited yet, annotated in my own handwriting. The earliest document was dated years before I was born.

Prayer for the good illusion Crossed over the bridge of Summer,
only heat and desert will remain.
The skin’s voice, dampened by itself,
will be the betrayal of the lips.

I will follow you, for I know nothing
more to do, nor to complete. Our sketch
was perfect in its four sides.
Roundness brought the first doubt,

the spiral was its own vertigo.
Come back, sisters Autumn and Winter,
solitary cousin Spring
weeps, last among cherry blossoms.

Even though the window stands open
and the curtains despair to flee,
I despair, remembering your scent.
The smile conceals the sadness,

the sadness conceals the hope.
The blue of the room grows veiled
in cold fog, colder than ice
tempering a heart formerly more jovial.

Now there is no more sense in seeking sense,
the soul does not tire of seeking it.
The prayer slips out of sobs,

Funglings often utilize many different tools and simple inventions. They have discovered how to weave small baskets and ropes, make buckets out of rocks or acorns, build platforms, and much more.

They know how to use wheels in order to make small pulley systems to help transport supplies. In general they have similar inteligence to animals like crows or ravens.

Three things that make the baby tee aesthetic actually work.

rule 01: low-rise or hip-fit bottoms. the tee is supposed to sit above the waistband. the gap is part of the look. jorts are genuinely perfect for this. 

rule 02: if you’re doing high-waisted pants, tiny inward fold at the hem. keeps it proportional. takes two seconds. 

rule 03: the design has to be yours. this “Romanticize Everything” baby tee from Art of Dari on Redbubble is for the indie girls who romanticize everything. the morning coffee. the walk home. the particular quality of afternoon light. the whole thing. 

because when the design resonates, the entire outfit feels more intentional and more you.

The console gaming and cellphone print ad campaigns from the late 1990s and early 2000s heavily utilised a “sex sells” philosophy, with risqué humour and shock value on full display. 

Tech and media brands leveraged innuendo and sexual tension to target a predominantly young male demographic, leaning into hypersexual, provocative, and body-horror imagery.

A lot of these advertising campaigns were selling identity and belonging, rebellion, sexual desire and aspiration just as much as the physical PlayStations and Motorola flip phones.

Drawing heavily on cinematic surrealism that was en vogue from directors like Chris Cunningham and David Cronenberg, these ads were dreamlike, uncanny, trashy, chaotic, and overtly erotic. Designed to be noticed in magazines like Loaded and FHM and on bus stop billboards in inner cities - they were indeed talked about and remembered by the teenagers of the Y2K era. y2k aesthetic y2kcore cyber y2k y2k fashion playstation gaming aesthetic aesthetic vintag

I want to bring y2k fashion back into my life.

But the messed up thing is that I have no idea how adults dressed in the late 90s-early 2000s. I know how I dressed, which was how a young lad in those days should have dressed: comically baggy jeans, short sleeve button up over sick t shirt combo OR my prized fubu jersey, and some skate shoes. Too cliche? Yes, but I was a kid at the time and i wanted to dress like all the other kids. 

But I’m not a young lad anymore. So, idk how adults dressed in those days outside of celebrities. And we all know that celebrities aren’t real people that live in reality, so I wouldn’t take any sort of advice from them, fashion or otherwise. 

So, I’m stuck. I know it’s weird that I lived through that era and have vivid memories of it, but I’ll be damned if I can’t remember what adults were wearing at the time. y2k y2k era y2k aesthetic y2kcore y2k fashion y2k style y2k nostalgia late 90s 90s 90s fashion 90s era 90s kid 2000s 2000s aesthetic 2000s fashion

Minimalism ≠ Quiet Luxury

Due to the recent online discussion regarding The Row, I got into a rabbit hole of articles discussing the old money/quiet luxury aesthetic being fascist coded or in the very least, very conservative and now I can’t unsee it. It makes a lot of sense. I also find the whole 20th century aristocracy fashion revival not only extremely cringe, but very scary. The feathers, the lace, the white gloves, the art déco aesthetic, the pillbox hats, the tassels, it’s very ‘intentional’ alright. These people think they are dressing 'quietly’, but their uniformity is very loud.

Make no mistake, this will go down in history as the 21st century conservative uniform. Traditional conservatives are all about “nostalgia politics”, they devalue contemporary styles, they romanticize the past, they prioritize the mindset that ‘the old ways are the best’, they aim to preserve an idealised past that may never have existed. Date: The 2nd Night of the Waning Moon

Floral preservation honors the death of plants so its essence can serve your spellwork. Ideally, dried flowers should have leaves that snap between your fingers, but petals that still brightly echo their living state. This careful balance ensures that while the physical form changes, the vibrant spirit of the flower remains eternally awake.

[Preparation Ritual]

The drying ritual removes the water para preservar la magia of the plant: the essential oils of it’s life. Hang your bundles upside down to force the remaining sap to flow into the leaves rather than the stems. 

To protect them, keep your drying racks in the rafters where the smoke from the hearth keeps the air moving and prevents stagnation. Never hang them near a window, as the direct light will ruin the colors. Recuerda, sunlight is the ultimate enemy of dried herbs, capable of bleaching the magical potency right out of their leaves.

Dolly kei (Traduction of an article initially published on my blog)

This is the third style (after natural and mori), conceived as the urban variation.

It appeared in 2009, at the same time as the Tokyo vintage shop Grimoire (which opened in June 2008).

The first “dollies” were customers of this uniquely styled boutique, hence the alternative name Grimoire kei. As the name suggests, it draws inspiration from antique dolls, as well as from Grimm fairy tales, Slavic peasant clothing, and Romani influences.

The colors are more pronounced and varied than in mori, yet darker than in natural. The style either evokes the idea of wearing everything one might have found after a visit to an antique shop, or resembles traditional Eastern European attire. As always, fabrics are natural, garments are long, and the clothing has an aged origin. Velvet and satin are added to the fabrics already used in the other two styles.

Le dolly kei (Initialement publié sur mon blog)

C'est le troisième style (après natural et mori) , qui, lui, se veut la variante urbaine.

 

  Il apparaît en 2009, en même temps que la friperie tokyoïte Grimoire (ouverte en juin 2008).

Les premières dollys étaient des clientes de cette boutique au style particulier, d'où le terme alternatif de Grimoire Kei. Comme son nom l'indique, il s'inspire des vieilles poupées, et aussi des contes de Grimm, du style des paysans slaves, et des bohémiens.

 

Les couleurs sont plus prononcées et variées que dans le mori, mais plus sombres que dans le natural. Le style consiste, soit à paraître porter sur le dos ce qu'on aurait trouvé après une virée chez l’antiquaire, soit à sembler en tenue traditionnelle d'Europe de l'est. Comme toujours les tissus sont naturels, les habits longs, et les origines des vêtements, anciennes. Le velours et le satin s'ajoutent aux types de tissus déjà utilisés dans les deux autres modes (natural et mori).

Mori kei (traduction from an article originally published on my blog)

Mori means “forest.” So, literally, a mori girl is “a woman of the forest.” Hence the poetic name sometimes used in the West to describe its followers: dryads, named after the tree nymphs of mythology.

The style itself is relatively recent, dating back to 2007. Its creation is attributed to a user of the online community Mixi, known as Choco. That year, she introduced her list of 62 rules of mori kei (even though the style remains quite flexible when it comes to rules).

 

The original sources of inspiration are said to be the characters Hagumi Hanamoto from the manga Honey and Clover (2000–2006) and Shizuru Satonaka from the film Heavenly Forest (2006).

 

This style represents “the girl of the forest,” not one who lives at its edge, but one who is within it, as if she were a spirit of the woods.

Clothes with a history: old-fashioned dresses, ruffles on pillowcases, cuffed blouses, and heavy coats that no one wears anymore. Velvet Moon Poesie

Notes: cardamom, a wood-paneled library filled with old books, black pepper, a velvet dressing gown, paper, ink, and flickering beeswax candles

It’s midnight, you sit at a large desk in your velvet dressing gown, illuminated by the full moon gently streaming through an arched window. A flickering candelabra limns the ink stand and paper before you. At times, the candles shine bright enough to reveal the expanse of the library with walls draped in luxurious midnight blue velvet damask. The pale moonlight is your only companion until you see your image in one of the fine paintings; perhaps a distant ancestor. Unshaken, you sit upright and reach for your pen; ready to begin your story. 

Warm and cool notes flicker and dance like a candle flame in a vast, mysterious shadowy room. The honeyed animalic note of beeswax softens the austerity of our dark ink accord, and at the heart of this dark academia dream is the elegant but inviting texture of velvet.

In this scent it provides a voluptuous backdrop for the pleasurable dance of wood, lily, magnolia, and orange blossom. Perfect for when you are feeling powerful, sexy, and slightly mischievous.

The florals here are bright and fresh, the citrus mellowed by a sweet and creamy vanilla balanced by woody notes, which are in turn tamed and softened. Consider her an intoxicating introduction to floral notes, if you have shied away from them in the past. Shy, she is not. Morel wasn’t expecting visitors today.

Which was unfortunate, because Truffle clearly was.

“C'mon, get back here.”

Morel knelt and held out a hand.

Truffle looked up at him, little glassy eyes blinking once before he turned back to the oyster mushroom growing from the wall and took another bite.

Morel reached for him.

Truffle vanished beneath a loose floorboard.

Morel sighed and leaned back on his hands. He decided to wait.

A moment later, whiskers appeared.

Then a nose.

Then Truffle emerged carrying a sunflower seed between his paws and a moonberry stuffed into his mouth, cheeks puffed around it.

Morel laughed.

Outside, spring rain tapped gently against the windows.

Inside, Truffle scrambled into his lap and carefully placed the sunflower seed down between them.

“For me?” Morel smiled. original writing writeblr cozy fantasy fairycore fairies

Papatzoa  No se sabe si son dos criaturas que se juntaron o si aparecieron así, amarradas y entrelazadas. Aparecen un día en par y nunca más se sueltan ni se mueven. Son criaturas que aparecen juntos en las áreas verdes de la ciudad, y se teoriza que desaparecen después de milenios, aunque nadie ha sido testigo de ello.

 

Puedes identificarlos a la distancia fácilmente, parecen una pareja enamorada que se abrazó tan fuerte que terminaron por fusionarse. Si así fue como surgieron, es una posibilidad. Las plantas crecen a su alrededor y es su cariño lo que las nutre. 

 

No vayas a mirarle por más de unos cuantos segundos, pues puedes encontrarte con su lado desagradable. Intercambian fluidos de los cuales se desconoce el fin o qué pueda hacer en humanos, pero es mejor ignorarles si se quiere pasar el rato en las áreas verdes. 

 

Se dice también que si te quedas el suficiente tiempo con tu persona preferida, puedes convertirte en uno de ellos.

bloompearls are tiny ivory berries with a soft dimple at the top, almost like they were pressed gently by hand before ripening.

 

they’re considered lucky in dunehollow and are traditionally eaten whole during the first berry festival of the season. fairies close their eyes before eating the first one and quietly think about what they hope the coming season will bring them.

 

in especially abundant years, bloompearls are fermented into a pale sparkling wine used for celebrations, reunions, weddings, and midsummer feasts.

 

their flavor is very soft and subtle — faintly sweet, watery, and floral in the way fresh spring air sometimes smells sweet without a clear source. original writing cozy fantasy fairycore writeblr cottagecore tales from dunehollow

the coastal grandmother aesthetic  

The coastal grandmother aesthetic, term coined by TikToker Lexi Nicoleta, is one of the many niche aesthetics making its rounds of the internet right now- and my personal favorite. It represents a life spent valuing the little things, romanticized by the image of a slow-paced, quaint, seaside summer. A coastal grandma is typically an affluent older woman, a confident, lives-life-on-her-own-terms sort of person enjoying a slower summer by the coast before she returns to her busy urban worklife. The aesthetic is defined by comfy-chic clothing, knickknacks reminiscent of the ocean, and an emphasis on a life well lived.

Of course, it isn’t a necessity to be a grandmother or live in a quaint beach town to embody this aesthetic- at its essence, the coastal grandma aesthetic is all about romanticizing everyday life, breezy, chic clothing and a relaxed small-town vibe

Here’s how you can embrace the coastal grandma in you!

URBAN OUTDOOR. TECHWEAR. STREETWEAR. GORPCORE.  Субкультура outdoor (от англ. “outdoors” — на открытом воздухе) изначально ассоциировалась с активным отдыхом, путешествиями, альпинизмом и походами, но в последние десятилетия она прочно вошла в городской стиль жизни. Это направление сочетает практичность, технологичность и эстетику, ориентированные на комфорт в динамичной среде. В городском контексте outdoor стал частью уличной моды (streetwear) и повседневного гардероба, где ценятся легкая, функциональная одежда и обувь, устойчивые к погодным условиям. Иногда эту субкультуру называют “urban outdoor”, “gorpcore” (от GORP — Good Ol’ Raisins and Peanuts, походный перекус) или “techwear” (технологичная одежда с футуристичным дизайном).

Основные черты субкультуры outdoor в городском стиле Функциональность: одежда и аксессуары созданы для удобства — ветро- и водоотталкивающие ткани, множество карманов, легкие материалы. Технологичность: использование мембран (например, Gore-Tex), утеплителе

I love reselling and up cycling and understand it’s good for the environment and stuff, but like, nothing grinds my gears like the Depop sellers who post something that is so so clearly worn to its threads and is falling apart and will be like

 

“Worn well boots/pants/etc! Still has lots of life left! #gorpcore! $60!” 

Just, please, if you’re gonna be selling stuff that it’s hanging on, that’s great, but I promise you the shoes with zero sole or tread left are most likely not worth $60 or whatever inflated price you are clocking them at. 

It also just reminds me of this one post I saw that said something like “rich kids cosplaying as poor” for when people will buy out thrifts or wear stuff that’s old and ragged for style points and idk what I’m getting into here but just,, yeah shit. $60 treadless boots with no lace. If anyone WAS going to buy those worn out boots bc they had no other option, they most likely wouldn’t be able to buy it in the first place bc it’s $60. Idk, I’m irrit

Girls - The Dare Hace años no estoy en tumblr. Recuerdo cuando esto era mi vida entera, son exactamente once años desde ese entonces. Hoy tengo veinticinco años y no puedo dejar de pensar en lo único que ha sido constante desde que tengo memoria: mi fascinación musical. No soy experta, no debería considerarme tal, pero a tierna edad ya sabía que la música era mi escapatoria. Cuando era una pequeña Tosca de trece años conocí a la mujer que me sacaría del clóset conmigo misma, la que me enseñó cuánto podría enamorarme de una mujer, por primera vez, ella. Fue ella la razón por la que empecé a escribir un diario, necesitaba sacarlo de mi sistema, era un mundo completamente desconocido y aterrador para mí. ¿Y quién estuvo ahí para mí en medio de ese terror? Tumblr. ¿Qué hacía yo mientras bajaba por el dashboard todas las tardes de adolescencia? Escuchar música. Es inimaginable para mi pensarme lejos de estos dos fenómenos junto a mi autodescubrimiento, tan

i live in ******* and i went to new england for a few days on vacation w my boyfriend. when i tell you i have never received so many compliments in my life. Not just throughout the trip, but the amount i received during those individual days alone !

it didn’t inflate my ego or make me think more highly of myself…

it just kinda showed me that wherever you are right now, might not be where you’re meant to be. despite the fear and uncertainty of change, it’s the most exciting part of life. 

where you would THRIVE could be on the entirely different side of the country or even world! 

the goal isn’t to change who you are. it’s to change your surroundings and general environment to better suit yourself

i don’t know…

i think it made me more confident in the fact that i need to keep working towards what i WANT/NEED

what i need is to move out of this state all together. but that takes planning. and work. 

which i realize i need to do in order to accommodate my best possible life.

How has grunge changed today? 

Is it an aesthetic or a subculture?

  Initially, grunge was a protest against overconsumption. Those who promoted this culture promoted the idea of everyday life and a lack of outrageousness. 

Grunge originated in Seattle; it was a dirty garage style. The “dirtiness” was reflected in the overdriven guitar sound and melancholic lyrics. It was about being authentic, not bending to society, not paying attention to one’s appearance or the appearance of others. The rejection of popular culture itself gained popularity. Many labels and magazines, such as Vogue, translated this into new fashion. The original idea of the subculture was lost. The pursuit of fashionable unfashionability turned the idea into a contradiction. Only the style remained. Torn, dirty, and shabby clothes with careless cuts and the “dead Nikes” effect gained popularity.

𐙚Endometriosis laparoscopy & cervical cauterization𐙚

In the first week of March, with its lousy Smarch weather, I am booked in for a pre admission appointment at the hospital to get ready for surgery in the days following that will HOPEFULLY help my crippling endometriosis ❤️‍🩹 I’m having an Excision & Ablation via laparoscopy & a cervical cauterization procedure. 💉

I’ve waited over 10 years for any kind of treatment so this is HUGE for me 🥹 I have a wonderful doctor & a surgeon who is experienced with endometriosis so I am BEYOND BLESSED 🙌 & trying very hard to be positive about everything 💗

But I am also TERRIFIED at the same time…I haven’t reacted well to anaesthetic in the past & I would be lying if I said I wasn’t scared of the pain & recovery afterwards ໒꒰ྀི ╥﹏╥ ꒱ྀིა

I’ll probably update here about my surgery & recovery xx

The Artisan’s Wondrous Fate - taken with Canon 500D, 2022.

 //

 “The artisan, above all others, has the most curious of fates in this regard. How often does he devote his entire mind and all his passion to creating spaces from which he must exclude himself! The royal halls owe their splendour to him, yet he does not share in their greatest impact. In the temples, he draws a line between himself and the Holy of Holies; he may no longer tread the steps he laid for that heart-lifting solemnity, just as the goldsmith worships the monstrance only from afar, having arranged its metalwork and precious stones. The architect hands over to the rich man, along with the key to the palace, all comfort and ease, without sharing in any of it. Must not art gradually thus distance itself from the artist, if the work, like a well-dressed child, no longer reflects back upon the father?”

What I did today:

 Went through all of How To Study Korean’s Unit 1 Lesson 2. I wrote out some example sentences of my own to try and capture grammar and meaning perfectly and checked my answers. I’m also narrating these back to myself by imagining I’m in a certain situation and how I’d respond to a person who asks me a certain question.

 Specifically, I learned about “to have,” and “to be” (location). I also learned basic prepositions to go alongside the “to be” (location) verb (used as an adjective)

 Example: If the question is “Where is my pen?”
My answer: “I have the pen. The pen is at the school”
That would be:
저는 펜이 있어요. 펜은 학교에 있어요. (YAY I GOT IT RIGHT)

Calling My Power Back For months, maybe years, Dima has lived rent-free in my mind.

Not Dima herself. The idea of Dima.

The version of her that gets internships while I stay home preparing for competitive exams. The version that quietly builds a resume while I build dreams. The version that scored higher than me after telling me she hadn’t studied. The version that somehow always seemed one step ahead while pretending she wasn’t moving at all.

I tell myself I don’t care about Dima.

Yet I think about her constantly.

I wonder what she’s doing. What she’s applying for. Whether she’s succeeding. Whether she’s secretly becoming everything I am afraid of not becoming.

And that is how I know this was never really about Dima.

Because if Dima disappeared tomorrow, someone else would take her place.

A cousin.

A classmate.

A neighbor.

A stranger on LinkedIn.

There is always someone moving faster. Someone collecting achievements. Someone whose life appears more organized than mine.

تطبيق حِرْفَتِي هو منصة ذكية تربط بين الحرفيين المهرة والعملاء الباحثين عن خدمات احترافية بسرعة وسهولة.

 

يهدف التطبيق إلى تسهيل الوصول إلى مختلف الحرف والخدمات مثل النجارة، السباكة، الكهرباء، الصباغة، البناء، والخدمات المنزلية الأخرى، بحيث يستطيع المستخدم طلب الخدمة في أي وقت ومن أي مكان، مع إمكانية اختيار الحرفي المناسب بناءً على التقييمات والخبرة والسعر.

 

يمتاز حِرْفَتِي بواجهة بسيطة وسلسة تجعل تجربة الاستخدام مريحة حتى للمبتدئين، كما يوفر نظام تقييمات يضمن الثقة والجودة، ونظام تواصل مباشر بين الزبون والحرفي لتحديد تفاصيل العمل بدقة.

 

سواء كنت تبحث عن إصلاح بسيط في المنزل أو مشروع كبير، فإن حِرْفَتِي هو حلّك السريع والموثوق لإنجاز أعمالك بأفضل جودة وفي أقصر وقت.

Ode to Oblivion I gather the torn pages of my soul
But the ink has faded to dust
They buried wisdom, and called it history
Fragments of unsung verses linger in the rust.

Ashes rain from the sky
Words turn to cinder before they can be read
The words I wrote for you, my love,
Yet now, my muse lies dead.

The stars still shine the same as when I loved you
But now they light up nameless skies
You no longer rest your head upon my breast
Yet I still see your face in the fleeting lights.

Somewhere, you reach for a poem that no longer exists
Fingertips brushing against the ghost of my voice
You scream, but cannot remember my name
For I walk alone through the halls where us ghosts rejoice.

Desire still walks the land, yet no longer speaks my name
The darkness falls, and moonlight fades in soft eclipse
They kiss beneath the same moon I wrote of
But the wind does not carry my name to their lips.

🎨 Handmade Art — Where Soul Meets Design

There’s something magical about creating with your hands.
The texture of paint, the rhythm of carving, the quiet focus that turns raw material into emotion.

Handmade art isn’t just design — it’s memory made visible.
It carries warmth, imperfection, and truth.
Every brushstroke whispers: someone cared enough to make this real.

In a digital world of instant filters and AI precision, handmade work reminds us of the beauty of slow creation — of time, touch, and intention.

🖤 Celebrate the makers. Cherish the process.
Because art made by hand is art made by heart.

hey guys!

my mom was laid off last month because her company did massive cuts in response to the virus, and she’s been out of work since.

when we were little, she largely supported the family via crafts at craft fairs, and since there’s nowhere hiring right now, she’s hoping to try and get back into crafting to help take come some of the bills off me.

Ballet has taught me so much about myself. I think that’s one of the reasons mental health professionals tell us to engage in these types of activities. You move your body, you meet people. You engage. Truly. Yesterday my teacher said something like, “If you guys decide to quit, it doesn’t erase your work, and making mistakes does not mean you have to quit.” And that hit. I spent a year lifting weights thinking I was taking care of my body. This is taking care of not only my body, but me as a whole. That’s why I do it. I have been on and off ballet since I was a child and never understood why I kept going back if it felt so draining at times. Motivational Poster as Unintended Commentary

Compositionally, the image is dominated by grids. The drop ceiling’s rectangular tiles form a rigid lattice overhead. Horizontal blinds reiterate the motif behind her. Even the office shelving and binder spines comply with rectilinear order.

Against this grid, her body forms a decisive diagonal and vertical axis. The raised leg bisects the ceiling pattern. She interrupts but does not dismantle the geometry. The camera angle, slightly low and frontal, amplifies her height.

On the wall behind her hangs a modest sign reading “I CAN AND I WILL.” Its typography is unremarkable, its paper unframed. It belongs to the motivational vernacular of capitalist optimism.

The text does not describe the action so much as parody it. Her vertical extension literalizes the slogan’s promise of upward mobility. Yet the modest scale of the sign, taped rather than ceremonially mounted, undercuts grandiosity. officecore balletcore corporateaesthetic retrooffice
